item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images used in this article are non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). The first image cannot be sourced and thus is to be assumed to be non-compliant. The second image comes from here and is All Rights Reserved. The third image is from National Geographic as shown here and is All Rights Reserved. The fourth image comes from here and must be bought to be used.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The writing itself is good, and made me read it to the end. Is weird in the right 5-style… but there's a couple expungements that feel tacked in.

if infection rates exceed 96%, SCP-2456-1 is predicted to [DATA EXPUNGED].

That adds nothing relevant at all but a forced 'look, spooooooky' feel. Same with the one of the corpses grabbing people to pull something redacted out. If it's an anomalous thing, we should know what, why and how. If it's a normal body part, is pointless to redact it…

Overall, still +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great skip and def +1. I did notice a typo: under the SCP-2456-1C tab, "casualties" has been misspelled as "causalities". Love all the work that has obviously gone into this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The "Exposition note that explains everything at the end" is a trope older than this site even, but the skip is so well done that I look over it and give it a resounding upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think it's intended to be. I love how overarching this particular mythos is. From Alagadda/SCP-701 and SCP-035, to the entire Sarkic/CotBG fight, to the Antimemetics tales, to the whole Class of '76 canon, the whole Fifthist shebang, to even Kalinin's proposal and it's associated skips.

It makes my head ache pleasantly just thinking about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never expected the positive feedback

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me a lot of Yeats. The Secret Rose or the Second Coming. Especially the cyclical destruction aspect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looks good, but draws just a few too many parallels with Cthulhu for my taste.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The contest seems to have provoked quite an effort to link up a variety of canons, and I am quite supportive of anything Fifthist. I think I was just about ready to upvote before I read the last two collapsibles, but those dragged mightily, never quite coalescing into either a realistic insight into (what is in effect) psychosis, or giving a consistent picture of what Fifthism means in this context. That's what makes Fifthism hard, of course, but while I admire the effort and applaud a lot of the execution, the lack of punchiness in the ending let this down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh, I totally thought this was going to be the thing from CASE COLOURLESS GREEN, when getting to the final addendum. Not identical (or even derivative) in the end, but the similarities are there.

item.title: Grammatical Errors
item.content:encoded:
I wasn't sure if these were intended or not, so I figured I would just comment.

The first addendum: "The man simply needed time to itself". You refer to the man as him right afterwards, so consistency would be nice.

The second addendum: "Even as I write. They whisper to me [unintelligible]". This is a bit confusing, it's an audio file, why would he reference writing? Maybe I missed something?

I think there were more, but I lost them, oops. I like the concept! The second addendum felt a bit too verbose with it's explanation of the gamma, maybe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noted. Thanks for the heads up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very well done. Most of the piece moves along splendidly, and there's an excellent buildup between the individual pieces. My only major complaint, is that Robespierre's diary drags a touch, I feel it's the weakest portion here. Given the history and spread of this thing, I'd prefer a a wider variety of documents, and I think that's doable. I'm also going to Nth the complaints regarding redaction as well, and there's one or two small bits where the tone drops you could fix:

+ show block
– hide block

Maximilien was but an ordinary β-instance

I'd nix 'but'.

immobilizing them and reaching into their throats, only to pull out their [DATA EXPUNGED].

and 'only' here.

Other than that, this is an excellent addition to the mythos, and makes for an easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, I haven't seen that much expunging since series III became a thing. Not necessarily bad, though, and the fifth tab of the thingy got my heart going. Overall, I'm not too sure what it is I just read, but I certainly enjoyed reading it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! And I've never heard of a skip that's a "city in the sun", so probably not. I admit though; I did put several references to other SCPs, although I tried to stray away from cross-linking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the notes. I'll be fixing the hymn once the contest is over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting. I think I got all the references, but I don't recognize the body in the water. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting way to tie all these various cults and religious movements together and to the Fifthist GoI. You do a particularly nice job of world building here.

I am going to echo what Cimmerian has previously said, though, in that you go a little heavy handed on the blackboxes and expungements. Those seem like they would have been interesting details that really would have added a nice spice to the narrative you are telling here. Oh well.

Either way, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I gotta say, while I feel the expungements and blackboxes hurt your story more than help it (in that they generally avoid adding some context to certain sections and in several locations they cause you to lose interesting details), the surrounding story is good enough to make up for those problems.

Easy +1 and an interesting addition to one of my favorite and, at least recently, underused GOIs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
corrections to corrections
to corrections to corrections to...

and some notations

But’s given the heart to its sole son

Is "but's" a contraction for "but has"? It ought to be but hast

I think "but hath" might be the more correct one there.
Also, hymns sometimes make extreme contractions like "but hast" to "but 'st" or even "b'st" for the sake of flow or fitting all the words into a verse (especially in translations) so that's something to keep in mind for the rest of these.

You alone are but their lifetime, a candle in the dark

Why the switch to second-person plural pronouns here?

Correction would be "Thou alone art their lifetime, a candle in the dark"
(the "but" doesn't really make grammatical sense there to begin with, so I left it out)

Thou cycles through the sky, sending visions from the heavens

Thou cyclest

I'd suggest a total rework into "In thine cycles through the sky, thou sendest visions from the heavens"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is an excellent entry in the Fifthist canon, and may actually codify this somewhat nebulous movement's beliefs/MO. I will however point out the grammatical errors in your Hymn:

+ show block
– hide block

Thou claims the world for its own, according to thy desire

Thou claimest

But’s given the heart to its sole son

Is "but's" a contraction for "but has"? It ought to be but hast

The stars and the Earth command to thy wishes.

It seems the sense in the verb "command" is reversed here.

You alone are but their lifetime, a candle in the dark

Why the switch to second-person plural pronouns here?

Thou cycles through the sky, sending visions from the heavens

Thou cyclest

Thou lives in the deepest drenches of the Great █████████ Sea

Thou livest, though dwellest is more likely in this construction. Is "drenches" an error for "trenches"?

Thou perished amongst the harlots; in the Fifth day of the solstice,
And the Fifth Year of the Summer Harvest, you suffered.

You switch back from second-person singular (thou) to second-person plural (you) in the same sentence here. Also, though not incorrect, Thou didst perish may be a more likely construction, as might thou didst suffer.

Thou shall live on to indulge the next world with thine divine antipathy!

Thou shalt, thy divine

Excellent work all around!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And this is why I should get my eyes checked.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty phenomenal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Um as a matter of fact, I'd say that the original translation for the character in the middle is correct. It seems to me that the word is a "令", which do mean "order/command", and it makes much more sense in this context than a "今", and can form a coherent phrase with the first two words.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well done, well structured, and the connection to the Fifth Church doesn't feel shoehorned in for extra weirdness (which is impressive, since I feel Fifthism is one of the hardest GOIs to write for, given their penchant for nonsensical, over the top ranting).

For my own amusement, is this connected to the city of the sun in… Uhhh… I forget which number?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oops. I thought I had them written down correctly, embarrassed to say it turned out to be the opposite. I'll be fixing the mistake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Still going through and digesting the article, but I have to highlight this small point.

Flag of the Taiping Rebellion. The two characters in the outer border stand for "King", while the inner one stands for "Order".

Are you sure? “天朝” translates to the Celestial Empire, which is a term for China in general (due to the Chinese emperor being the 'Son of Heaven'). As for “今”, it translates to "now".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My first ever SCP, and an entry for hiscon2017! I've poured blood, sweat, and tears into this one article, and honestly, its been a crazy experience. Even with the limited time frame, I still think I accomplished much. Thanks to everyone who read it in the chat, and those who gave me pointers. Love it or hate it, you guys have helped me formulate my ideas better.

I want to acknowledge rimple for the incredible amount of help he gave me. Not only did he let me steal the code for the glitch text, he also cleared up inconsistencies in the text and polished the article. Honestly, without his advice and TREMENDOUS help with editing, this entire thing wouldn't have even been possible. Just, wow. I honestly didn't think people would spend so much of their time selflessly helping others. Thanks for everything man :)
